title: Guidewire Software Posts Better-Than-Expected Q3 Report, But Stock Drops
author: Erica Kollmann
description: Guidewire Software reported Q3 earnings of $0.82 per share, beating analyst expectations of $0.74, with revenue of $372.54 million exceeding the $355.95 million estimate. Subscription and support revenue grew 35% year-over-year. Despite the strong results, the stock dropped 14.66% to $129 in after-hours trading.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the company delivered better-than-expected financial results with strong revenue growth (35% in subscription and support) and earnings beat, the stock experienced a significant 14.66% drop in after-hours trading, indicating investor disappointment despite operational success. This disconnect between strong fundamentals and negative price action suggests market expectations may have been even higher or concerns about future guidance.

title: Earnings Volatility Watch: 10 Stocks Set For Big Moves This Week, From Nuclear Power To Whiskey Maker
author: Piero Cingari
description: Options markets are pricing significant implied moves between 15.92% and 21.71% across 10 stocks reporting this week, spanning AI infrastructure suppliers, nuclear power, cybersecurity, and consumer staples. The largest moves are expected for Netskope (21.71%), Brown-Forman (20.51%), and Planet Labs (18.95%), with major mega-cap companies like Broadcom, CrowdStrike, and Palo Alto Networks also reporting amid continued AI infrastructure demand.

title: Insurance Analytics Market Size to Hit USD 72.39 Billion by 2035 | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Insurance Analytics Market is valued at $17.60 billion in 2025 and expected to reach $72.39 billion by 2035, growing at a CAGR of 15.19%. Growth is driven by fraud detection, AI adoption, cloud computing, and alternative data sources like telematics and IoT sensors. Software components and cloud deployment dominate the market, while SMEs and government agencies represent the fastest-growing segments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a leading market player in the growing insurance analytics market with strong demand for AI-powered analytics solutions
Identified as a key player in the expanding insurance analytics market benefiting from cloud deployment and digital transformation trends
Identified as a key player in the expanding insurance analytics market benefiting from cloud deployment and digital transformation trends
Major player in insurance analytics market positioned to benefit from growing adoption of enterprise analytics solutions
Highlighted with recent 2025 development expanding AI-powered aerial imagery analysis capabilities for property insurance risk assessment
Listed as a leading market player in the high-growth insurance analytics sector
Identified as a key player benefiting from strong market growth in insurance analytics and digital transformation

title: Naitiv Launches as the First AI-Native ServiceNow Consultancy
author: Unknown
description: Naitiv, a new AI-native ServiceNow consultancy founded by former Thirdera executives, has launched with the acquisition of two ServiceNow partners (Cloudworks and Inspira Systems). The firm will initially focus on AI-driven digital transformation for the Property & Casualty insurance industry, positioning ServiceNow as an orchestration layer to modernize insurance operations and connect legacy systems.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ServiceNow is positioned as a core strategic platform and orchestration layer for enterprise transformation. The launch of a specialized AI-native consultancy dedicated to ServiceNow implementations indicates growing demand and confidence in the platform's capabilities for enterprise-scale modernization.

Cognizant is mentioned only as the acquirer of Thirdera (the previous employer of Naitiv's founders). The article does not indicate any direct business relationship or impact on Cognizant from Naitiv's launch.

Guidewire is mentioned as a system of record that Naitiv will integrate with ServiceNow. This represents a neutral mention as a complementary technology partner rather than indicating competitive threat or opportunity.

Salesforce is mentioned as one of the legacy systems that will be connected through ServiceNow's orchestration layer. This is a neutral mention indicating integration rather than competitive positioning.

title: The Trade Desk, Circle, And Venture Global Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (March 2-March 6): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Ten large-cap stocks were top performers during the week of March 2-6, 2026, driven by strong earnings, raised guidance, bullish analyst calls, and strategic catalysts. The Trade Desk led with a 26.7% gain following OpenAI advertising discussions and insider buying, while Intuit surged 19.25%, Guidewire jumped 17.99% on better-than-expected results, and LyondellBasell soared 17.65% on analyst upgrades.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest weekly gainer at 26.7% driven by potential OpenAI advertising partnership discussions and insider stock purchase, indicating strong market confidence
Gained 10.51% with analyst price target raised from $90 to $100, though Mizuho maintains Neutral rating
Rose 15.11% during the week, benefiting from defense sector momentum
Jumped 19.25%, indicating strong market performance and investor confidence

Soared 17.65% with multiple analyst price forecast raises
Gained 11.43% driven by crude oil price increases from U.S.-Iran tensions and new LNG purchase agreement with Trafigura

Jumped 17.99% following better-than-expected Q2 results, raised FY26 guidance, and analyst price target increase from $175 to $200
